competitions.product: Win This ROLEX Datejust 36 ref. 16233
competitions.product_info: Chrono Win Competitions How it works Winners 0 Collectors Watch Competition Win This ROLEX Datejust 36 ref. 16233 Time Left to End: 5 Days 19 Hrs 50 Mins 47 Secs 1.00% SOLD 594/600 Tickets Remaining Enter Now winners Value: £6500 The Rolex Datejust 36 ref. 16233 is a circa-1990s example with a 36mm stainless-steel case, 18ct yellow-gold fluted bezel and steel-and-gold Jubilee bracelet. It features a striking blue vignette diamond dial, sapphire crystal and quickset date with Cyclops magnifier, powered by Rolex’s automatic calibre 3135. The watch is supplied with its original box and papers and combines classic five-digit Datejust proportions with one of the most distinctive dial configurations from the era. Collectors Watch Competition - Guaranteed Winners - Transparent Draws End Date: July 19, 2026 10:00 pm - Draw Date: 19/07/2026 10:30PM Collectors Watch Competition Win a ROLEX Datejust 36 ref. 16233 ROLEX Datejust 36 ref. 16233 — a standout two-tone Datejust from one of ROLEX’s most recognisable and wearable eras. This example features the highly desirable blue vignette dial with factory diamond hour markers, a 36mm stainless steel Oyster case, 18ct yellow gold fluted bezel, sapphire crystal, Cyclops date window and the classic steel and yellow gold Jubilee bracelet. From a collector’s perspective, the dial is what sets this watch apart. Standard champagne, silver and white Datejust 16233 examples are widely seen, but the blue vignette diamond dial gives this piece a much more distinctive personality. The gradient effect, fading darker around the edge of the dial, brings serious 1990s ROLEX character and gives the watch far more presence than a standard two-tone Datejust. The 16233 also sits in an important sweet spot for collectors. It retains the classic 36mm Datejust proportions and traditional two-tone luxury aesthetic, while benefiting from modern usability features such as sapphire crystal, quickset date and the highly regarded ROLEX calibre 3135 automatic movement. This is exactly the type of Datejust collectors increasingly look for: recognisable, wearable, liquid, and with a dial configuration that makes it stand out from the ordinary examples on the market. This example is in excellent, unpolished condition with a pristine “Mk 3” dial . #32 - The Cartier Paris Tank Louis 78086 18k Solid Gold The History— The Example The Cartier Tank Louis is one of the defining wristwatch designs of the last century. First introduced in 1922 as a refinement of the original Tank, the Tank Louis established the elegant proportions that have come to define Cartier, and has been worn by artists, royalty and cultural icons ever since. This reference, the 78086 , is one of the most desirable vintage iterations. Crafted in 18k solid yellow gold , it features the highly sought-after Paris-signed dial , a detail that collectors have long admired for its rarity and historical significance. This particular watch is presented in excellent, unpolished condition , retaining crisp case lines and a pristine Mk 3 dial . The beautifully balanced proportions make it equally elegant on both men and women, wearing with the understated sophistication that has made the Tank Louis such an enduring classic.
